step_by_step_reasoning: We identify two distinct AWS accounts (Account A and Account B) separated by a boundary line. Account A contains two public subnets with NAT Gateways and two private subnets with EKS clusters. Account B contains a WAF, an ALB in a public subnet, and an EKS cluster in a private subnet. An on-premises data center is represented at the bottom, and a developer user is represented at the top. We will map these nodes and logical flows representing internet outbound traffic, on-prem access, and Account B ingress.

graph.name: GoDaddy Secure Multi-Account EKS Architecture
graph.notes: Focuses on zero-trust multi-account isolation, 24-hour EKS node rotations, and strictly controlled lateral movement with no VPC peering.

nodes.name: Developer
nodes.notes: Initiates application creation via the public cloud portal.

nodes.name: NAT Gateway AZ1
nodes.notes: Outbound internet gateway for EKS workloads.

nodes.name: EKS Workload AZ1
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Rotates nodes every 24 hours using golden AMIs.

nodes.notes: Outbound internet gateway for redundant EKS workloads.

nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Rotates nodes every 24 hours using golden AMIs.

nodes.name: AWS WAF
nodes.notes: Protects public endpoints in Account B.

nodes.name: ALB
